230864.explanation: More direct influence for residents in important decisions, for example according to the Swiss model.

230865.explanation: Support close to residents strengthens livability and prevention.

230866.explanation: The administration should be organized based on quality and tasks, not on a fixed number.

230867.explanation: Young people must be able to structurally participate in policymaking.

230868.explanation: A strong civil service organization is needed for good service delivery. We want to be less dependent on expensive external agencies.

230869.explanation: We choose targeted, sustainable growth that fits Winterswijk.

230870.explanation: This requires tailor-made arrangements with entrepreneurs and residents.

230871.explanation: Developments in the countryside must fit nature, landscape, and livability. Tailor-made solutions are needed.

230872.explanation: New business activities must be future-proof and responsible.

230873.explanation: Farmers are already overwhelmed by regulations from other governments.

230874.explanation: Sustainable recreation must be possible to maintain livability but must be balanced with landscape and tranquility.

230875.explanation: Accessibility is important for the residents of Winterswijk, but costs and implementation also matter. The polluter pays.

230876.explanation: Citizen initiatives increase involvement and greening.

230877.explanation: Sustainability is the norm for the future economy.

230878.explanation: Mowing less strengthens biodiversity. However, it must not come at the expense of traffic safety. So, tailor-made solutions are needed.

230879.explanation: Careful location selection is better than a general ban.

230880.explanation: Winterswijk already contributes above average to the sustainability of our energy needs through solar energy on land. The location where wind turbines might be possible is too close to vulnerable nature. We maintain the moratorium.

230881.explanation: If you close off the market, then parking is no longer possible. And that is undesirable for our residents and entrepreneurs.

230882.explanation: This strengthens culture and recreation. A shelter for bad weather is truly an asset for Winterswijk.

230883.explanation: It is a long-term goal. Greening and cooling of the city center are necessary for livability. It must be affordable and desirable per location. This is currently not a priority for us.

230884.explanation: Sustainability of the rural area deserves support.

230886.explanation: Integration is realized by participation, for example through work and sport, but more money is not a goal in itself.

230888.explanation: Humanity and legal responsibility come first. The asylum seekers’ center in Winterswijk functions well and is an example for the rest of the Netherlands.

230889.explanation: Local media strengthen democracy, but unfortunately the subsidy is handled by the central government.

230890.explanation: Regional language strengthens identity and connection.

230892.explanation: Sale is not a goal in itself and can only be discussed with a better alternative.

230894.explanation: Increase equal opportunities. The youth have the future.

230899.explanation: This increases the feeling of safety and addresses small issues before they become big.

230901.explanation: The municipality makes agreements with the ABH-City Association (the entrepreneurs) about a good balance. The city center does not benefit from half-closed shopping streets. Additionally, center entrepreneurs also deserve peace.

230902.explanation: There must be a limit to opening hours.

230903.explanation: Desire of the police, but only where necessary and effective.

230904.explanation: We want fair and legally sustainable policy; returning students (for example) also deserve equal opportunity.

230905.explanation: Sympathetic, but a strict minimum limit of 30% can also hinder the feasibility of housing construction.

230907.explanation: This harms the housing market and tourism. Moreover, new holiday homes would then have to be built elsewhere in the rural area.

230908.explanation: First infill, then expansion. But both are needed, because infill has a limit due to the required space for other things like greenery, parking, and water storage.

230910.explanation: We support informal care and new housing forms, but this requires tailor-made solutions. Allowing a second home everywhere is not possible.

230911.explanation: But take into account the use of the Market for different purposes, so only in places where it is still possible.

230912.explanation: An information center is needed. This location could also qualify.

230913.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate, but it must also be affordable.

230914.explanation: The condition is that this is mentally and physically possible.

230916.explanation: Protection of low incomes remains necessary.

230917.explanation: Education increases sustainable opportunities, so priority should be given to education.

230922.explanation: 30 km/h everywhere does not do justice to differing circumstances. For example, 30 km/h is too fast on a residential yard.

230923.explanation: A level crossing solution is desirable. This improves traffic circulation and strengthens the social connection between the two halves of Winterswijk.

230924.explanation: A tunnel is not feasible at this location.

230926.explanation: The Vredenseweg is too narrow for higher speeds.

230928.explanation: An investigation would be nice, but we believe there is a high chance that the costs will outweigh the benefits. It is not a high priority.

230929.explanation: First, better investigate whether this is really necessary and feasible.

230930.explanation: First hear from the health services whether this is the right approach.

230931.explanation: Balance between health and entrepreneurial freedom. Smoking can also take place outside the terrace.

230932.explanation: First better investigate whether more information provision is useful and desired.

230864.explanation: Many political decisions are complex and contain nuances. In a referendum, such issues are often reduced to a yes/no question, causing important details, conditions, and consequences to be overlooked. Moreover, the municipal council is a reflection of the Winterswijk population.

230865.explanation: Voor Winterswijk supports a neighborhood-oriented way of working, collaborating with residents on important themes such as health, poverty, social interaction, etc. Voor Winterswijk focuses not on the number of staff but on cooperation and methods of working.

230866.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes it is temporarily possible to work with a maximum of 3 aldermen, but this is not structurally desirable because there is too much work. The final decision lies with the new coalition.

230867.explanation: Voor Winterswijk does not think a separate advisory council should be established, but strongly supports participation with young people. Therefore, Voor Winterswijk has many young candidates on its electoral list. Not talking about youth, but talking with youth.

230868.explanation: Voor Winterswijk critically examines the number of civil servants but recognizes that more tasks are being assigned to municipalities. Sufficient and well-qualified personnel are needed to perform all tasks.

230869.explanation: Voor Winterswijk currently has no ambition to attract companies from outside Winterswijk to the development sites Arrisveld and De Vlijt. At Veneslat, there is little vacancy, so a promotional campaign is unnecessary.

230870.explanation: Voor Winterswijk is not opposed to more shopping Sundays if the retailers' association desires it. Currently, this is not the case.

230871.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes this must be assessed per situation, due to the impact of a campsite on its surroundings. However, the municipality should look into opportunities for secondary activities at farms.

230872.explanation: Voor Winterswijk agrees with this. Because Arrisveld is close to Korenburgerveen, sustainability plays an important role in this area. Additionally, national regulations on this matter are increasing significantly.

230873.explanation: Voor Winterswijk cherishes the landscape and farmers of Winterswijk and is cautious about imposing extra rules that put pressure on them. National regulations already make things complicated enough.

230874.explanation: Voor Winterswijk does not rule out more overnight accommodations. Preferably, Voor Winterswijk wants the number of overnight stays to grow through tourists visiting Winterswijk outside the peak season.

230875.explanation: In practice, this statement means that costs must be borne by all residents via waste disposal charges. Voor Winterswijk supports the principle: the polluter pays.

230876.explanation: Voor Winterswijk welcomes such initiatives from residents. Doing this together as a neighborhood can also increase social cohesion within neighborhoods.

230877.explanation: Sustainable working is a broad concept. Voor Winterswijk does not want to impose it as a strict requirement because other considerations are also important. Companies are needed for jobs, maintaining facilities, and supporting associations. We do not want new polluting or nuisance-causing industries.

230878.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes that safety at unclear intersections should be a priority when it comes to mowing verges.

230879.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes that sufficient large solar parks have been approved in Winterswijk. Winterswijk has also more than fulfilled the regional objective for 'solar on land.' The preference is for solar panels on roofs. Small solar fields for personal use should be allowed according to Voor Winterswijk.

230880.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes Winterswijk does not have suitable locations for large wind turbines.

230881.explanation: Voor Winterswijk finds good accessibility of the center important. Voor Winterswijk believes measures should quickly be taken to reduce speed on the market to prevent antisocial driving behavior, for the sake of safety and livability in the center.

230882.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes this initiative fits the objectives of the existing subsidy scheme for local initiatives. A one-time amount is fine, but not structural support with public funds.

230883.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes the Whemerbeek in the shopping center should remain underground because otherwise, it would reduce parking spaces at the Wheme.

230884.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes farmers should be compensated for adopting different working methods. The national government must make financial resources available for this.

230885.explanation: Voor Winterswijk prefers awareness raising and positive stimulation over fining. Processing waste is becoming increasingly expensive for municipalities, making proper separation more important.

230886.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes the current situation is sufficient.

230887.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes safe and good housing is important for everyone.

230888.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes the asylum seekers' center in Winterswijk is working well. Good agreements result in relatively little nuisance. Something that works well does not need to be changed if there is no necessity. Every municipality must take its share.

230889.explanation: Voor Winterswijk holds the local broadcaster in high regard. Due to new national legislation, the broadcaster will receive funding from the government starting in 2028 to safeguard its independence. RTV Slingeland already receives extra funds for a smooth transition to the new legislation.

230890.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes that regional language and heritage contribute to the bond among residents.

230892.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes it is important to have a swimming pool available for residents.

230894.explanation: Voor Winterswijk finds it important to invest in our youth. Good education is the foundation for the future of our residents and must remain accessible to everyone.

230897.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes no more coffee shops are needed. The municipality does not need to facilitate drug use anymore.

230898.explanation: Voor Winterswijk values safety and decency.

230899.explanation: There must be sufficient capacity to enforce regulations. Voor Winterswijk values safety and decency.

230900.explanation: Voor Winterswijk supports having more facilities to securely park bicycles. This must be feasible and affordable.

230901.explanation: Voor Winterswijk is not opposed to more shopping Sundays if the retailers' association desires it. Currently, this is not the case.

230902.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes that entrepreneurs can perfectly well decide this themselves.

230903.explanation: Voor Winterswijk is of the opinion that camera surveillance is not necessary and should only be applied in cases of excesses.

230904.explanation: Voor Winterswijk would like people to have a social bond with Winterswijk. Exceptions can be made in special cases.

230905.explanation: Voor Winterswijk finds it important that sufficient affordable housing is built in Winterswijk and that there is a good mix of owner-occupied and rental properties.

230906.explanation: Voor Winterswijk supports the transformation of vacant buildings into homes. This helps prevent deterioration and decline. Voor Winterswijk does not support housing on the ground floors of shopping streets, as this detracts from the quality of the shopping center.

230907.explanation: Experiences elsewhere in the country show that the combination of permanent residence and recreational use in parks leads to problems. Moreover, the infrastructure is not designed for vehicles such as garbage trucks.

230908.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes that agricultural land should certainly not be excluded from housing development if it suits the location. This is already possible within the Environmental Vision Outer Area.

230910.explanation: The current housing task requires creative solutions, so it is good to be open to this in suitable locations. In the countryside, it should not spoil the landscape.

230911.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes that the Market could be greener, in line with the shopping streets. However, functions such as parking, the market, and the local festival on the market must be taken into account. These functions have priority.

230912.explanation: Voor Winterswijk considers Tuunterveld a logical location for housing expansion but believes that vacant buildings in the built-up area should first be transformed into homes to prevent neighborhood decline. First in-fill, then expansion.

230913.explanation: Voor Winterswijk supports the goal of Poverty-Free 2040 but disagrees with this statement because the income threshold for assistance in Winterswijk is already relatively high. While income support is sometimes necessary, it is better to invest in preventing poverty.

230914.explanation: Voor Winterswijk finds it important that residents contribute socially, provided they are able to do so.

230915.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes there are sufficient opportunities in the job market, also for the long-term unemployed. Subsidized jobs are therefore not necessary. Voor Winterswijk finds it important to invest in employment.

230916.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes the current situation works well and sees no reason to change it. Stopping this scheme would likely lead to increased use of other poverty schemes.

230917.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes that even people pursuing retraining should apply for jobs.

230920.explanation: Voor Winterswijk thinks there should be sufficient charging points distributed across Winterswijk.

230922.explanation: Voor Winterswijk agrees with this, for the safety of road users and livability for residents.

230923.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes this is unnecessary because recent research shows the added value is very limited and the investment is high.

230924.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes this is unnecessary because recent research shows the added value is very limited and the investment is very high.

230925.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes the Vrijheidspark should remain closed to motorized traffic. However, we should look at smarter ways to reach the right destination faster. A new use for the old post office and the Jacobus church could create more activity in the Vrijheidspark.

230926.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes the maximum speed limit in built-up areas should return to 30 km/h. This improves safety for road users and livability for residents.

230928.explanation: Voor Winterswijk sees a complete bypass as desirable but not realistic. However, Voor Winterswijk believes bottlenecks on the bypasses must be addressed, as traffic is increasing. For the distant future, we must keep an open eye on what is possible.

230929.explanation: Voor Winterswijk disagrees with this statement because previous experience showed there is too little interest in the night train, while train transport is expensive.

230930.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes that instead of facilitating drug use through testing locations, the municipality should focus on enforcement, discouragement, and reducing drug use.

230931.explanation: Voor Winterswijk supports smoke-free places but believes it is up to entrepreneurs to assess this regarding their terraces.

230932.explanation: Voor Winterswijk believes prevention is better than cure.

230933.explanation: Informal caregivers must be well supported by the municipality. They need a good safety net.

230864.explanation: Residents can give their opinion on all subjects, for example by contacting the faction or speaking at a meeting. Preferably in advance rather than afterwards with a referendum. Complex decisions are oversimplified too much. The CDA favors letting elected representatives make the choices.

230865.explanation: The CDA wants the municipality to be low-threshold, visible, and accessible in neighborhoods. This way, help requests, for example in support and upbringing, can be properly addressed and residents assisted.

230866.explanation: More and more tasks are coming to the municipality. In addition, we want aldermen to have enough time to visit organizations to hear firsthand what is going on. From April, Winterswijk will also get a new mayor who needs to get to know Winterswijk.

230867.explanation: The CDA initiated the children's council. We also consider a youth council a good idea if there is good representation and interest among young people. It helps to involve youth in politics and helps politics understand what young people find important.

230868.explanation: There must always be a critical look at the number of civil servants, and if less can do the job, the CDA supports that. But with more and increasingly complex tasks at the municipality, it is not realistic to do more work with fewer people.

230869.explanation: The CDA finds business activity important for Winterswijk. We do not currently see it as the municipality's task to actively seek new companies to settle in Winterswijk.

230870.explanation: The CDA aligns with the choice of the majority of entrepreneurs in the center. We prioritize the interests of local entrepreneurs. For the strength of Winterswijk's Sunday shopping, it is important that many stores are open during Sundays.

230871.explanation: Currently, under conditions, it is already allowed to start a minicamping at an agricultural business. The CDA wants to maintain this possibility. It also gives farmers more options to operate their businesses financially healthily. It must not cause disadvantages for neighboring businesses.

230872.explanation: Arrisveld will be a green workplace landscape. Large polluting companies do not belong in a green workplace landscape. New companies must therefore comply with the latest environmental standards.

230873.explanation: Because of our scenic landscape, having an agricultural business here is already more expensive than in areas with large contiguous land. Europe, the national government, and the province already impose enough rules. Locally, we impose no extra rules.

230874.explanation: The number of overnight places for the summer period does not need to increase. We do see room to extend the season for places that can be used year-round, so there is also more tourism in the winter period.

230875.explanation: Waste is not free. If everyone can 'free' deliver it at the recycling center, it means the basic waste fee goes up for everyone. The CDA chooses to make residents who produce little waste pay less. If you do have a lot of waste, you pay for it yourself.

230876.explanation: If residents want to do maintenance in their neighborhood for the same or a lower amount and thereby earn money, for example, for a neighborhood party, it is a win-win situation for everyone!

230877.explanation: Existing rules apply at existing locations. All companies are allowed to establish themselves there. However, environmental requirements are becoming increasingly strict. Companies at existing locations must also comply with these requirements.

230878.explanation: Mowing must be done frequently enough to keep the road safe and visibility unobstructed. Mowing less often may seem messier, but it is beneficial for biodiversity. Mowing more frequently also costs extra money, which then comes at the expense of something else.

230879.explanation: Winterswijk has already installed many hectares of solar panels on agricultural land. Therefore, we choose to use agricultural land for other purposes, such as extensification for Winterswijk farmers.

230880.explanation: We value sustainable energy and energy security. The options in Winterswijk are very limited, only around Masterveld. As far as opportunities exist, we want the wind turbines to interfere as little as possible with the landscape and for local residents to be compensated.

230881.explanation: We collaborate with hospitality sectors, shopkeepers, market vendors, and residents to create a better, more atmospheric design that remains attractive to visitors. By removing through traffic from the Market square, more opportunities are created and shopping becomes safer.

230882.explanation: The municipality has pledged one-time funding for the realization of the visitor center. The CDA supports this. It provides shelter during bad weather at a unique location in Winterswijk and is therefore good for tourism. The CDA does not want to pay an annual contribution to the visitor center.

230883.explanation: We want to make the Whemerbeek visible to limit flooding during heavy showers, combat heat stress, and make the area more attractive. An above-ground stream helps with this. However, it is not an end in itself and will be coordinated with other projects.

230884.explanation: Provincial and national subsidies are already available to help farmers engage more in nature-friendly agriculture. This is also a provincial responsibility. We do not consider it a municipal task.

230885.explanation: By sorting waste properly, we keep costs as low as possible. Incorrectly disposed waste results in extra costs for the municipality and thus for all of us. Therefore, it is fair to issue fines to those who repeatedly dispose of their waste incorrectly in extreme cases.

230886.explanation: The municipality already spends a lot of money on the integration of newcomers in Winterswijk. This is going well. There is no need to spend more money on this.

230887.explanation: If there are signals that housing is inadequate or complaints about nuisance, the municipality can conduct more frequent inspections. Good housing is primarily the responsibility of the employer and the landlord.

230888.explanation: Winterswijk has had a well-functioning asylum seekers center for decades. Setting an end date is unnecessary. Furthermore, there is an obligation to accommodate asylum seekers. Closing the current location means a new asylum center would have to be opened elsewhere in Winterswijk.

230889.explanation: Thanks to a CDA amendment, RTV Slingeland has received extra funding for several years. This will continue until they become a regional broadcaster. If demonstrably additional funds are needed to keep the local broadcaster operational until then, the CDA is positive about that.

230890.explanation: Dialect is a part of Winterswijk's identity. It is spoken less and less among young people. To keep the language alive in the future, it is good for all children to come into contact with it.

230892.explanation: We consider a swimming pool a basic facility and want the current situation to remain. The pool is also available for target groups, such as (patient) associations, which are not interesting for commercial parties.

230894.explanation: Education and tutoring are the responsibility of the schools. The extended school day for extra support should be available to all students but can also be organized through cooperation between schools.

230897.explanation: Winterswijk has one coffeeshop. That is sufficient. It is neither necessary nor desirable to have more coffeeshops in Winterswijk. Supervision would then have to be spread over multiple locations. Concentrating it in one location has advantages.

230898.explanation: According to the CDA, the police should enforce rules more often when they observe antisocial behavior and violations by anyone participating in traffic—not specifically or only people riding fat bikes.

230899.explanation: Cycling through shopping streets, inspections of businesses, long parking in the blue zone, waste dumping, and much more. There are still many issues where municipal enforcement officers could be more involved.

230900.explanation: By creating a safe place where people can securely park their (electric) bikes, we encourage cycling to the center instead of driving. Parking a bike is often faster than a car, especially on busy days. It is important to ensure the bike remains safely where it was parked.

230901.explanation: The CDA aligns with the choice of the majority of entrepreneurs in the center. We prioritize the interests of local entrepreneurs. For the strength of Winterswijk's Sunday shopping, it is important that many stores are open during Sundays.

230902.explanation: Due to public order, safety, (noise) nuisance, and enforcing these, it is better to regulate a maximum closing time.

230903.explanation: As long as there is no necessity, the CDA does not see a need for camera surveillance and no reason to invest heavily in it.

230904.explanation: The CDA has long supported a priority system for people who live here or have a socioeconomic connection. This way, someone who works here or has friends, family, or a club here can get a house sooner.

230905.explanation: There is demand for all types of housing in Winterswijk. Therefore, we must build according to demand with a good mix of social rental, mid-range rental, and owner-occupied homes.

230906.explanation: If there are (long) vacant offices and shops, the owner can invest in converting them into homes and recoup the investment. Winterswijk subsidies are not immediately necessary for this.

230907.explanation: The CDA wants to preserve recreational homes for recreation. The infrastructure (roads, street lighting, waste points) is not designed for permanent residence. Recreational homes are also used for recreation, an important sector for tourism.

230908.explanation: The CDA wants to prevent agricultural land from being easily sacrificed for other functions. However, the CDA does want to build 2,250 new homes. These cannot all be built within the existing built-up area.

230910.explanation: We consider a temporary caregiver’s home a good idea. An additional permanent home is, also for the neighbors, not a good idea.

230911.explanation: More greenery on the Market square provides more decoration and experience and helps combat heat stress. However, the different functions of the Market must be taken into account: of course the market itself, but also terraces and events like the Volksfeest.

230912.explanation: The CDA wants to build 2,250 new homes. These cannot all be within the current built-up area. When searching for a suitable location, Tuunterveld could be an option if it emerges as the best location in a study.

230913.explanation: The support threshold was raised in 2025 from 110% to 130% of the social assistance income. This is a substantial increase, allowing more people to benefit. Further increases would lead to divergence from neighboring municipalities and cost us much more money.

230914.explanation: If people on social assistance are able to perform socially meaningful work, the CDA prefers that they do so with a paid job. That way, they exit social assistance. Ultimately, this is better for both the individual and the municipality.

230915.explanation: People must be intensively guided toward employment or encouraged to pursue education.

230916.explanation: Forgiving municipal taxes is a good and simple form of support for people with low incomes.

230917.explanation: Through retraining, residents improve their chances of employment. By temporarily not having to apply for jobs, full attention can be given to studies. Ultimately, having a job is better for both the resident and municipality.

230920.explanation: We must ensure sufficient charging stations for electric cars, both in public spaces and at municipal buildings, based on demand. If a charging post is used frequently, an additional one will be added.

230922.explanation: To improve traffic safety, 30 km/h will be the standard speed limit within built-up areas, and roads will be designed accordingly.

230923.explanation: The effects of an additional crossing are too small to justify such a large investment. Slowly moving trains at the station can cause long waiting times at the railroad crossings on Dingstraat / Stationsstraat / Parallelweg.

230924.explanation: The effects of an additional crossing are too small for such a large investment (about 20 million). Also, a long tunnel section would obstruct the Stationsstraat/Dingstraat route because the intersection would disappear, possibly affecting the presence of the police station.

230925.explanation: The Freedom Park does not necessarily have to be reopened to cars. Without through traffic, it can remain more of a park. However, the one-way streets in the area should be reviewed and their driving direction adjusted to improve access to the center.

230926.explanation: The Vredenseweg is not a safe road, especially for cyclists. By reducing the speed and redesigning the road accordingly, it becomes much safer. Redesigning for 30 km/h also provides more space for greenery and parking.

230928.explanation: The positive effects of a complete ring road are limited. However, the existing ring roads and their associated roundabouts (especially the Corleseweg and Europalaan roundabouts) need to be relieved. Traffic flow currently gets stuck there.

230929.explanation: Regional train transport is regulated and paid for by the province. For the amount it costs for a limited number of Winterswijk residents (on average 2) who use it, we prefer to invest in transport that more people depend on. Going out in Arnhem for an evening is not included in that.

230930.explanation: The safest drugs are the ones you do not use. Do you still want to have them tested? Then you can already go to Enschede, Deventer, Arnhem, and Nijmegen. These are easily accessible locations if you want to use something safely.

230931.explanation: Ideally, we want a smoke-free generation and we want everyone to be able to sit on a smoke-free terrace. We want hospitality entrepreneurs to voluntarily choose a smoke-free terrace in return for paying less terrace tax (precario).

230932.explanation: We want everyone to be able to grow old as healthily as possible. Good information helps to know which (healthy) choices you can make yourself and what possibilities exist in Winterswijk.

230933.explanation: We increasingly rely on informal caregivers. Caring for your loved one can be rewarding but also intensive. To ensure informal caregivers occasionally have time off and a moment for themselves, we want more respite care: extra helping hands to temporarily take over the tasks of the informal caregiver.

230864.explanation: Residents must be able to participate in important choices in Winterswijk. That is why we only support a binding referendum with a clear yes-or-no question, where it is clear in advance what it concerns and the result truly counts.

230865.explanation: It's not about having more employees, but about working better in the neighborhoods. Residents want clarity, quick responses, and fewer rules. This can be done without extra staff in the neighborhoods, but by critically assessing what is needed to truly support residents.

230866.explanation: The municipal government must be clear and affordable. With three full-time aldermen, the executive board can function well, but it's not about the number of 'figures'. Sometimes having more aldermen means more clout, but it can also be about the number of hours worked. It is about quality of governance.

230867.explanation: Young people have good ideas about their future. With a youth council, they get a voice in local politics. That results in better decisions.

230868.explanation: The municipality needs enough people to properly help residents. We want fewer rules and paperwork, but not less service.

230869.explanation: A strong economy provides jobs and amenities. Winterswijk can present itself even better as a good place to live, recreate, and do business. That also helps the economy progress further.

230870.explanation: More shopping Sundays give entrepreneurs and residents more freedom. That makes the center livelier. This must, however, be done with consideration for livability and in consultation with ABH-City.

230871.explanation: Not every location is suitable for a campsite. Tourism must fit with nature and the surroundings. Therefore, tailor-made solutions are necessary.

230872.explanation: Sustainability is important, but not every company can immediately do everything sustainably. We want to encourage companies to take steps forward, not exclude them in advance.

230873.explanation: Farmers already have many rules. The municipality should not continually add new rules. We will continue to care for our nature and landscape.

230874.explanation: Tourism is important but must remain balanced. Too many overnight accommodations can disturb peace and nature. Quality is more important than growth, so tailor-made solutions are necessary. Where possible, we allow it.

230875.explanation: Completely free can lead to higher costs for everyone. We choose fair rates and clear rules.

230876.explanation: Residents often know best how to improve their neighborhood. The municipality should support such initiatives. That results in more greenery and engagement.

230877.explanation: We want to stimulate sustainability, but also allow room for development and innovation. Taking steps together works better.

230878.explanation: We mow where necessary for safety, but let nature do its work as much as possible. That is good for plants and animals. However, it is important that we manage our verges ecologically, meaning mowing and removing cuttings. Otherwise, we get more and taller weeds.

230879.explanation: Sustainable energy is necessary. We must carefully consider where solar panels fit. Not everything should be prohibited outright.

230880.explanation: Large wind turbines have a big impact on the landscape. That is why we are cautious here. Support is important, and preferably we designate one suitable search area (Masterveld). Wind energy can also contribute to reducing congestion in the electricity grid.

230881.explanation: Less traffic makes the center safer and more pleasant. That is good for shopping and staying. We take good accessibility into account.

230882.explanation: The Steengroeve is unique for Winterswijk. A visitor center strengthens nature and tourism. That is good for the region.

230883.explanation: This plan is very costly. We prefer to invest in housing, education, and care. That is currently the priority.

230884.explanation: Farmers are important for Winterswijk. We want to help them work more sustainably. That strengthens the landscape and future. However, the responsibility mainly lies with the province and the national government to support the transition, not the municipality.

230885.explanation: Properly sorting waste is the norm. Repeated improper behavior may lead to enforcement. If everyone sorts their waste well, everyone also pays less waste disposal tax. That is fair for everyone.

230886.explanation: Good integration helps people participate. That prevents problems later and strengthens society.

230887.explanation: Everyone deserves safe and good housing. The municipality must monitor this. That ensures fair conditions.

230888.explanation: We take responsibility for reception. Good agreements are more important than an end date. Stability helps organize it well.

230889.explanation: The local broadcaster connects residents. It provides local news and showcases our Winterswijk. That deserves support.

230890.explanation: Dialect is part of our identity. It is a beautiful addition to education and increases pride in the region.

230892.explanation: The swimming pool was recently extensively renovated with significant funds. Selling it now would negate the investment. Swimming education remains D66's highest priority.

230894.explanation: Every child deserves equal opportunities. Extra support helps with that and prevents setbacks. We focus on equal opportunities and strong education. Therefore, extra support for students who have setbacks fits with this.

230897.explanation: Banning does not solve problems. Regulating and monitoring works better. That is more realistic.

230898.explanation: Dangerous behavior in traffic must be addressed, including by residents causing nuisance on fat bikes. That increases safety for everyone.

230899.explanation: Visible enforcement increases safety. Community enforcement officers (BOAs) are nearby and approachable. That helps in neighborhoods and the center.

230900.explanation: Cycling is increasingly important. That is why we want good bike parking and space for bicycles. However, paid guarded bicycle parking does not fit the scale of Winterswijk.

230901.explanation: More Sunday shopping days give entrepreneurs and residents more freedom. That makes the center livelier. However, this must be done with consideration for livability and in consultation with ABH-City.

230902.explanation: Freedom is important, but so are peace and livability. Agreements remain necessary, as do options for possible late openings. But with the many residents in our center, their needs must also be considered, so 24-hour openings are currently not realistic for Winterswijk.

230903.explanation: Privacy is important. We only want to deploy cameras when there is a demonstrable necessity and other measures do not work.

230904.explanation: Housing shortages cannot be solved by exclusion. We need to build more for everyone. Many young people also temporarily move to the city to study and live in student housing. They are welcome there just as we welcome new residents.

230905.explanation: Affordable housing is necessary, also in new neighborhoods. The 30% social rental housing is an objective for us in Winterswijk.

230906.explanation: Tackling vacancy helps reduce the housing shortage. That is smart use of space and prevents deterioration. However, we critically assess whether homes should be built between shops or if we should maintain a continuous shopping area.

230907.explanation: That can cause problems for livability. It is better to build real homes.

230908.explanation: Housing construction is urgently needed. This can also be outside the core. However, it must be carefully integrated and preferably at Tuunterveld.

230910.explanation: If people have space in a garden or yard, a (temporary) caregiver’s home should be allowed. This ensures families can live together and help each other. For large plots, it should also be easier to split the parcel.

230911.explanation: Green spaces provide cooling and pleasant places to stay. That makes the center more attractive.

230912.explanation: This is a logical location for housing. Affordable homes can be built here. That helps those seeking housing.

230913.explanation: Some people currently fall just outside the rules. By raising the limit, we help more people. That provides peace of mind and certainty, especially given the growing group of workers living in poverty.

230914.explanation: Coercion does not help residents move forward. Guidance and trust do. That works better.

230915.explanation: For some people, work is not self-evident. Subsidized jobs help them participate. That is valuable.

230916.explanation: Debt forgiveness prevents debts and stress. That is humane policy.

230917.explanation: Education increases the chance of employment. There should be room for that. That is logical.

230920.explanation: Electric transport is growing. Charging stations are needed, but preferably mainly for bicycles. All charging stations will be placed smartly and clustered where possible to prevent all neighborhoods from being overrun by uncontrolled growth of charging points.

230922.explanation: 30 km/h is appropriate in many places, but not everywhere. Safety comes first.

230923.explanation: This attracts extra traffic. That is undesirable. We choose tranquility in residential neighborhoods. Furthermore, ProRail has indicated it will not allow a crossing. This means a tunnel must be built. We find the costs of 15 to 20 million too expensive and not worthwhile.

230924.explanation: This attracts extra traffic. That is undesirable. We choose tranquility in residential neighborhoods. Furthermore, ProRail has indicated it will not allow a crossing. This means a tunnel must be built. We find the costs of 15 to 20 million too expensive and not worthwhile.

230925.explanation: Car traffic in the center limits livability and causes extra congestion and unsafe situations. Priority is that access is guaranteed for emergency services, deliveries and the disabled. Other solutions seem more appropriate, such as reconfiguring one-way streets in the neighborhood.

230926.explanation: Many cyclists ride here and vehicles drive too fast for safe conditions. 30 km/h increases safety in built-up areas.

230928.explanation: A ring road can remove traffic from residential streets. That improves livability and safety.

230929.explanation: Good accessibility is important, also in the evening and at night. That helps young people and workers. Young people have little nearby. Therefore, we want them to be able to travel safely back from a night out.

230930.explanation: A testing location prevents health risks. That is sensible policy.

230931.explanation: Smoke-free is healthier for guests and staff. That fits a pleasant environment. However, this should be done gradually and in consultation with the hospitality entrepreneur. Consideration can also be given to part of the terrace. As long as this is not nationally regulated, we leave the decision to the entrepreneur.

230932.explanation: Prevention is better than cure. Education helps people make healthy choices. That lowers healthcare costs. However, this policy should not be patronizing. So we advocate clear choices on what is effective.

230933.explanation: Caregivers often become overburdened. Replacement care provides relief. That is urgently needed.

230864.explanation: Referendums are difficult instruments to use properly. Complex realities are simplified into 'for' or 'against'. We believe a strong, broadly representative council can make these decisions well on behalf of Winterswijk residents.

230865.explanation: Winterswijk is more than the center or its tourism. Livability starts with people in their own neighborhood. If our municipality wants to understand what is happening there to support the neighborhood well, we need more staff for this.

230866.explanation: There are many challenges in the coming years demanding our attention: nitrogen, housing, sustainability, and more. This requires strong governance, even if that means having more than three aldermen.

230867.explanation: Young people have an important voice in our future. They deserve to be taken seriously. This can, for instance, be done with a youth council.

230868.explanation: Of course, we must be critical of new ambitions, but reducing the number of civil servants means we can do less for our citizens.

230869.explanation: Winterswijk has a lively entrepreneurial culture. We are proud of our businesses, whether they started here or relocated here. The PvdA is not opposed to new businesses settling, although we find further promotion excessive.

230870.explanation: As far as the PvdA is concerned, the municipality follows the wishes of the city center entrepreneurs. After all, they know best what they need or consider necessary.

230871.explanation: Farmers who want to open a campsite or other facility near their farm can always count on a positive attitude from the PvdA. However, not every part of the Winterswijk landscape is suitable for a campsite. That must also be taken into account.

230872.explanation: We see the new Arrisveld work landscape as a true business park of the future. According to the PvdA, polluting industry does not belong there.

230873.explanation: Farming in Winterswijk is not easy. Moreover, current national legislation already demands a lot from farms. We believe the municipality should not impose additional burdens on top of that.

230874.explanation: According to the PvdA, the municipality of Winterswijk should focus mainly on extending the tourism season. This means not more locations, but more overnight stays per location.

230875.explanation: Experience shows that making waste disposal free leads to larger amounts of waste. Considering our sustainability goals, we find this difficult to reconcile.

230876.explanation: In our view, the 'right to challenge' is a good solution. If a neighborhood can maintain the greenery better and cheaper, the PvdA fully supports that.

230877.explanation: Some industries are inherently polluting. However, we already have a relatively large number of them in Winterswijk. The PvdA prefers companies of the future to come to Winterswijk.

230878.explanation: Public green spaces must remain well-maintained; that is the most important thing. If some biodiverse verges can be mowed less often to preserve ecology, that is a good reason to refrain from mowing for a longer period.

230879.explanation: Winterswijk is making good progress in its energy transition. Unfortunately, we still have a significant way to go. Therefore, Winterswijk cannot afford to ban solar panels on land. Moreover, this goes against regional and national agreements.

230880.explanation: Solar panels on roofs and on land are not enough to become energy-neutral. Wind turbines require less space than solar panels. For this reason, under strict conditions, the PvdA sees wind turbines as a possible addition to our local energy generation.

230881.explanation: To make our center greener, safer, and more pleasant, in our opinion, cars on the Markt have become subordinate.

230882.explanation: The municipality can make a one-time financial contribution to the Steengroeve Museum. For the annual costs, the museum itself must come up with a plan.

230883.explanation: In terms of heat stress and especially experience in the center, we see the Whemerbeek as a nice solution.

230884.explanation: Farmers deserve a livelihood in Winterswijk. The current way of farming is unsustainable. We are happy to help with a transition to nature-friendly agriculture.

230885.explanation: Issuing fines requires a lot of enforcement, which in turn costs a lot of money. We see the solution for waste separation more in 'after-the-fact' sorting of waste during processing.

230886.explanation: It is important that newcomers quickly become part of our society. Better integration helps with this.

230887.explanation: Everyone deserves a good place to live, including labor migrants.

230888.explanation: Winterswijk has the best asylum seekers center in the Netherlands. We are proud of this. Therefore, from the PvdA's perspective, closure is out of the question.

230889.explanation: Good journalism is important for a strong democracy, including locally. We would like to see RTV Slingeland merge into a regional broadcaster to better guarantee this. If extra costs need to be incurred for this, we are willing to pay.

230890.explanation: Education in the Netherlands (including primary education) is already expected to do very much. The PvdA mainly wants schools to focus on equal opportunities and basic skills.

230892.explanation: Public facilities must remain publicly owned. Selling the Jaspers swimming pool, in our view, would only lead to more expensive admission tickets.

230894.explanation: Every child deserves a fair chance to make something of their life. This sometimes requires unequal investments to achieve equal opportunities. In the PvdA's opinion, this investment is more than worth it.

230897.explanation: The PvdA sees little necessity for a new coffeeshop. Should an application be submitted for this, we will review it at that time.

230898.explanation: Everyone must obey the law. If this is not the case, people should be confronted with that. We find extra police supervision for this excessive.

230899.explanation: We have too few police officers in Winterswijk. Therefore, community enforcement officers (BOAs) are a necessary addition.

230900.explanation: The PvdA sees cycling as a solution to increasingly busy traffic. People should not be afraid that something will happen to their expensive (electric) bike. A guarded bicycle parking facility is a solution.

230901.explanation: As far as the PvdA is concerned, the municipality follows the wishes of the city center entrepreneurs. After all, they know best what they need or consider necessary.

230902.explanation: We see no necessity for this here. Should there be a necessity, this is possible in good consultation.

230903.explanation: In some locations, we understand the demand for more camera surveillance, although it doesn't need to be everywhere.

230904.explanation: Everyone has the right to housing. The most important thing is that we can meet this need. Therefore, we must build substantially. If there are too few social rental homes in the short term, Winterswijk residents should have priority, according to the PvdA.

230905.explanation: At the moment, we are building too few social rental homes, which means people with a small budget can no longer continue living in Winterswijk. The solution is simple: build more social rental homes.

230906.explanation: The PvdA believes we should actively combat vacancies. In some places, this can be done by converting them into homes. We would like to contribute to this.

230907.explanation: Holiday homes are not built as permanent residences. As a result, they often lack many facilities. Making them suitable costs the municipality and the residents a lot of money. The PvdA prefers to build suitable homes immediately.

230908.explanation: Winterswijk has a large housing shortage. We must build for this, including on agricultural land.

230910.explanation: Homes are built for at least 50 years. Therefore, careful consideration is required about how and where. Situations can be imagined where building a home in the garden offers a solution. Good consultation is required here.

230911.explanation: Greening our center reduces heat stress and improves the appearance. A greener Markt makes Winterswijk a more attractive shopping area.

230912.explanation: The PvdA mainly wants to build houses. A new neighborhood or neighborhood expansion is necessary for this. We care less about where this ultimately should be. Tuunterveld is among the possible locations.

230913.explanation: Children living in poverty should still have the chance to play sports, make music, or join a club. It is good for everyone to find connection with society. If the municipality can help financially with this, we will do so.

230914.explanation: It is valuable to be and remain involved in society. However, there are good reasons why people sometimes cannot work temporarily. We do not want to irresponsibly pressure these people.

230915.explanation: Basic jobs offer a low-threshold opportunity for people who have been unemployed for a long time to return to working life.

230916.explanation: Tax waivers help some people avoid falling into real poverty. The most important thing is that this helps the people themselves. Additionally, it prevents possible higher costs for the municipality.

230917.explanation: If retraining leads to a sustainable, valuable contribution to society, we should not hinder this by forcing people to apply for jobs.

230920.explanation: The electric car is probably the most important means of transport of the future. The municipality must embrace this development.

230922.explanation: The PvdA attaches great importance to traffic safety, especially for cyclists. There are clearly identifiable roads where, in our opinion, 30 km/h should be the speed limit. Roads with a separate cycle path may retain the 50 km/h speed limit.

230923.explanation: Traffic studies show that this connection is not a solution for our traffic flow. Therefore, we prefer to spend the money on other matters.

230924.explanation: Traffic studies show that this tunnel is not a solution for our traffic flow. Moreover, it costs many millions to realize a tunnel. We prefer to spend this money on other matters.

230925.explanation: The Vrijheidspark is an awkwardly designed traffic situation. Opening it to cars causes other problems again. Therefore, we prefer to see another solution.

230926.explanation: The Vredenseweg is a very narrow access road. At the moment, it is unsafe for cyclists. Given the limited possibilities to widen this road, we see it being reduced to 30km/h.

230928.explanation: Traffic flow should take place as much as possible via a bypass. This will make the roads within our town safer and allow people to reach their destinations faster.

230929.explanation: The night train is not one of our priorities. Previous research indicated it would not be successful.

230930.explanation: The municipality should not set up such a test location alone. There is no test location for drugs anywhere in the Achterhoek. Therefore, the PvdA advocates for an Achterhoek-wide collaboration for such a test location.

230931.explanation: We follow national policy on this matter.

230932.explanation: Healthcare costs are soaring both nationally and locally. Prevention is better than cure. This also applies to healthy living advice.

230933.explanation: Research shows that informal caregivers are under increasing pressure. The government retains a duty of care, also in informal caregiving.

230864.explanation: We value participation, but especially at the start. Residents know their village and neighborhood best and should be able to share their ideas, suggestions, and concerns while plans are still being made, not just once the plans are already set. The municipal council is elected to make balanced decisions.

230865.explanation: We do not choose more civil servants, but better service. The municipality must work smarter, create fewer rules, and help residents faster. Money should go to solutions in the neighborhood, not to a growing administrative apparatus.

230866.explanation: The number of aldermen should fit the tasks. Quality and effectiveness are more important than a fixed maximum. Sometimes extra capacity is needed to properly address housing construction, safety, and accessibility. The VVD is ambitious, so we do not limit ourselves to a maximum of three full-time aldermen.

230867.explanation: Young people deserve a clearer voice. A youth council increases involvement and ensures that policy better aligns with all plans we want, can, and must make around, for example, housing, education, and youth leisure.

230868.explanation: It's not about fewer people, but about working better. Residents should be helped quickly and well. This requires a smart, service-oriented organization. However, we must always keep monitoring where in the organization there are too many or too few officials and dare to make adjustments.

230869.explanation: Winterswijk must be attractive for businesses and workers. By actively promoting, we strengthen our economy, create jobs, and maintain facilities such as the SKB, clubs, and our lively center. That is investing in growth and a future-proof municipality.

230870.explanation: The VVD wants fewer rules. Entrepreneurs should have more freedom to decide when they are open. We are open to considering one or more additional shopping Sundays. These create a lively center and attract more visitors. The municipality should facilitate, not unnecessarily restrict.

230871.explanation: Farmers must have room to operate and diversify their income. Small-scale campsites strengthen tourism, provide extra income, and keep the countryside livable, as long as they fit well within the environment.

230872.explanation: Sustainability is important, but we do not want to exclude companies. When a company is a genuine addition to Winterswijk for good reasons, we must always remain open to that. Entrepreneurs should have room to establish themselves, grow, and gradually become more sustainable.

230873.explanation: Farmers deserve trust and as few unnecessary rules as possible. At the same time, nature, living environment, and health must be protected. We choose tailored solutions and cooperation through ongoing partnerships, not generic prohibitions or new stacks of rules.

230874.explanation: Tourism strengthens the local economy. More overnight accommodations provide extra income, employment, and vibrancy, while the character of Winterswijk must be preserved. We do this not by increasing the peak during the high season, but by extending the season.

230875.explanation: Waste processing is becoming increasingly smarter and more efficient. We believe in clear rules and personal responsibility. A small fee prevents abuse and ensures costs are fairly distributed, so the system remains affordable and reliable for everyone.

230876.explanation: We appreciate initiatives from residents, but structural subsidies are not necessary. The municipality must ensure good basic management. Those who want to do extra work in the neighborhood deserve support in rules and collaboration, not immediate financial arrangements.

230877.explanation: Sustainability is important, but we do not want to exclude companies. When a company is a genuine addition to Winterswijk for good reasons, we must always remain open to that. Entrepreneurs should have room to establish themselves, grow, and gradually become more sustainable.

230878.explanation: Mowing must be balanced. Too little is messy and unsafe; too much is bad for nature and biodiversity. We choose tailored approaches: neat where necessary, natural where possible.

230879.explanation: A good mix of sustainable generation through wind and solar is important. We fully invest in solar panels on roofs and industrial sites. We are cautious with agricultural land, but a ban goes too far. We choose well-considered decisions, not a general prohibition.

230880.explanation: A good mix of sustainable generation through wind and solar is important. Sustainable energy is necessary, but large wind turbines greatly impact the landscape and residents. We want to assess each plan individually, with genuine input from residents.

230881.explanation: The center must remain easily accessible for residents, entrepreneurs, and emergency services. As long as there is no good alternative for traffic through and around the center, we will not close the Markt. Fully closing it causes detours and extra congestion elsewhere. We choose accessibility and safety.

230882.explanation: The Steengroeve is a unique attraction. A good visitor center strengthens tourism, education, and the local economy, attracts more visitors, and fosters pride in our heritage. With this museum, we also offer tourists something during bad weather. This extends the tourism season.

230883.explanation: Uncovering the Whemerbeek is very costly. At present, Winterswijk faces many other challenges to which we give priority. We prefer to invest the money in housing, education, infrastructure, and facilities that residents use daily.

230884.explanation: In Winterswijk, we work with the agricultural sector in an area-oriented approach. We collaborate with farmers on how they can continue operating while adapting to national and European regulations. Not with individual subsidies, but with tailored solutions and future perspectives.

230885.explanation: Waste processing is becoming smarter and more efficient. We believe in clear rules, good explanation, and personal responsibility. We do not choose to punish people. Instead, we focus on clear government policy and fair cost division.

230886.explanation: Integration is about participation: learning the language, working, and being part of society. This requires a good approach, not automatically more money, but clear expectations and effective guidance. Winterswijk does this well and will continue to do so.

230887.explanation: Good housing is important for safety and livability. Rules exist for a reason. The municipality must strictly check whether employers provide decent and safe housing for their employees, to prevent abuses and avoid overburdening neighborhoods.

230888.explanation: Winterswijk has taken responsibility in this national challenge for years. We adhere to agreements with the national government and believe it is now up to other Achterhoek municipalities to take their responsibility. Winterswijk does enough and will continue to do so, but now it's up to others.

230889.explanation: A strong local broadcaster is important for a well-informed society. RTV Slingeland connects residents and brings local news. Additional support is possible, provided professional work is maintained and the broadcaster remains future-proof. We continue discussions with RTV about quality and agreements.

230890.explanation: The dialect is part of our identity. Attention to it in schools strengthens culture and pride, as long as it is supplementary and proper Dutch remains the foundation.

230892.explanation: Swimming pool Jaspers has struggled financially for years and regularly runs deficits. A renewed approach is needed to keep it open in the future. Cooperation with a commercial party can provide more certainty and sustainability. See Marveld in Groenlo.

230894.explanation: Education is the responsibility of schools and the national government. The municipality must primarily ensure good buildings and conditions, not structurally interfere in the curriculum.

230897.explanation: The local situation is decisive. We look at safety, nuisance, and livability. Decisions about these require careful consideration, not automatic or simple choices.

230898.explanation: Nuisance undermines livability and causes feelings of insecurity. More supervision and enforcement make it clear that rules apply to everyone and contribute to quiet and safe residential neighborhoods.

230899.explanation: Visible enforcement increases safety and livability. More community enforcement officers enable quicker responses to nuisance, illegal parking, and unsafe behavior. This gives residents the feeling that rules are enforced and that the municipality is there for their safety—and rightly so.

230900.explanation: Safe storage is important, but a guarded storage facility is costly. We do not exclude this outright but first look at smart solutions such as good lighting, supervision, and design. If that is not sufficient, a guarded storage can still be considered.

230901.explanation: The VVD wants fewer rules. Entrepreneurs should have more freedom to decide when they are open. We are open to considering one or more additional shopping Sundays. These create a lively center and attract more visitors. The municipality should facilitate, not unnecessarily restrict.

230902.explanation: Entrepreneurs themselves know best when they can be open. More freedom in opening hours strengthens nightlife and the economy, while maintaining clear agreements on nuisance and safety.

230903.explanation: Cameras can help prevent and solve crime. They increase the feeling of safety in the center and support the police and enforcement. Of course, we remain vigilant about respect for privacy and clear rules for the use of footage.

230904.explanation: Winterswijk residents must be able to continue living here. At the same time, we must adhere to rules and assign fairly. We choose a good balance between opportunities for our own residents and a fair system. New Winterswijk residents also deserve a chance to contribute.

230905.explanation: A fixed percentage means more rules and higher demands for developers. This makes building more complicated and less attractive, causing projects to delay or not proceed. With this housing shortage, we must speed up construction and choose tailor-made solutions instead of adding extra barriers.

230906.explanation: This can be a solution for vacancy, but not every building or street is suitable. Shopping streets, for example, should remain shopping streets. We assess each situation to see if it makes sense and give developers space to quickly start realizing much-needed housing.

230907.explanation: Holiday homes are meant for recreation and tourism, not permanent residence. Permanent living can put pressure on livability and amenities and leads to unclear situations. We solve the housing shortage with real homes, not stopgap solutions.

230908.explanation: To solve the housing shortage, thousands of homes must be built in Winterswijk. This cannot be done only within the existing built-up area. There is too little space, and densification means high-rise flats and lengthy procedures. Expansion onto carefully selected agricultural land is therefore necessary.

230910.explanation: A small second home, such as a caregiver's unit, helps families care for one another and offers extra living space. With clear and simple rules, this can be done without nuisance and contributes to organizing care nearby and reducing pressure on the housing market.

230911.explanation: The Market must remain primarily an open and usable square for events, terraces, and accessibility. This is part of the strength of the center. Greening is important but must fit the function of the square and not come at the expense of space and vibrancy.

230912.explanation: The Tuunterveld is a logical place for new homes. Building here allows us to realize hundreds of additional homes relatively quickly, from rental to ownership, and close to amenities. To solve the housing shortage, we cannot avoid having an expansion location unless we opt for towering high-rises.

230913.explanation: Help must be aimed at those who really need it. Raising the threshold constantly makes it less targeted and less customized. We prefer person-centered solutions and guidance towards work so that people can progress independently.

230914.explanation: Those who can, should participate. Socially useful work provides structure, increases chances of employment, and contributes to society. At the same time, health and personal circumstances must always be taken into account.

230915.explanation: Subsidized jobs often solve the problem temporarily. It is better to invest in real jobs, training, and cooperation with entrepreneurs so people sustainably find work and do not remain dependent on subsidies. Winterswijk has many social organizations and entrepreneurs. We must seize this opportunity.

230916.explanation: Remission must remain possible for those who truly cannot pay. At the same time, the system must remain fair and affordable. Good guidance and debt assistance are at least as important as whether remission is granted or not.

230917.explanation: Education is important, but so is contact with the labor market. A temporary exemption can sometimes help, but always with the goal of returning to work as soon as possible and not staying outside the labor process for long.

230920.explanation: There must be enough charging poles for electric drivers, but not everywhere at the expense of parking spaces for those who cannot afford an electric car. Placement must be smart and demand-driven, together with residents and entrepreneurs.

230922.explanation: A general 30 km/h zone would turn Winterswijk into a dormitory town. The center would become congested, accessibility would worsen, and flow for public transport and emergency services would come under pressure. They have already expressed concerns themselves. Therefore, we choose tailor-made solutions on main roads. Safety and accessibility matter.

230923.explanation: This solution costs millions. New connections like this must demonstrably contribute significantly to accessibility and safety. So far, we have not seen sufficient evidence and therefore first look at better use of existing infrastructure.

230924.explanation: This solution costs millions. New connections like this must demonstrably contribute significantly to accessibility and safety. So far, we have not seen sufficient evidence and therefore first look at better use of existing infrastructure.

230925.explanation: The Freedom Park is currently poorly accessible and designed in a traffic-wise illogical and unsafe way. This must be improved. If research shows that the square can be safely opened to traffic with adjustments, we will seriously consider that option.

230926.explanation: The Vredenseweg is an important access road. 30 km/h in itself is not a big problem, but traffic-calming measures like speed bumps and narrowings are. This slows emergency services and puts bus connections under pressure. We choose tailor-made solutions and flow on the major important roads.

230928.explanation: Further research must clarify this. For now, we are positive. A bypass may improve flow and relieve residential neighborhoods. It strengthens accessibility for residents, businesses, and emergency services and contributes to a future-proof infrastructure.

230929.explanation: A night train increases accessibility for young people and visitors. It makes Winterswijk better connected to the region and strengthens its appeal as a place to live, work, and socialize. For us, this is part of new negotiations with regional public transport services.

230930.explanation: Drug use is a reality. As far as the VVD is concerned, we fully focus on preventing drug use. However, doing nothing means looking away. Testing possibilities reduce health risks and provide better insight into dangerous substances. This contributes to safety, especially among youth.

230931.explanation: We stand for freedom of choice for entrepreneurs and visitors. Too strict rules limit that freedom. Protection of non-smokers remains important through good agreements and clear zones. We focus on prevention of tobacco addictions among youth through school campaigns.

230932.explanation: Good education helps people live more consciously and healthily. That prevents healthcare costs and strengthens well-being. The municipality can support this with practical and accessible information.

230933.explanation: Caregiving is valuable and stems from involvement and personal responsibility. However, it is not feasible to let large parts of caregiving be taken over by care organizations. Personnel shortages in healthcare are significant. We cannot blindly rely on that.

230864.explanation: List 8 believes that the voice of the citizen is always important. A referendum can help the municipal council make important choices. For us, it is crucial that citizen participation actually leads to decisions in the interest of our residents!

230865.explanation: According to List 8, the resident comes first. Neighborhood-oriented work offers much room for improvement. Therefore, it is a task to look together with residents at the right support in the neighborhoods. 'Looking out for each other.'

230866.explanation: List 8 values the visibility and quality of aldermen more than their number. For example, if better quality for residents can be achieved with 4 aldermen, we are open to that discussion.

230867.explanation: Youth will play an important role for List 8 in the coming council term. We see that too much is talked about youth instead of with youth. It is important that they make choices now for the future. 'Who has the youth, has the future.'

230868.explanation: List 8 believes that new technologies can yield administrative savings in the municipal organization. Also, matters can be tackled jointly in the region. Better and solution-oriented work for the citizen!

230869.explanation: List 8 believes more effort should be made to attract SMEs, new entrepreneurs, and start-ups. A healthy mix of different companies. This may mean that very large space users can be encouraged to move to regional business parks.

230870.explanation: If center entrepreneurs want this freedom, List 8 will certainly encourage it. Entrepreneurs usually know best how the retail sector works and is structured. If this contributes to a lively and vibrant center, we are in favor.

230871.explanation: If there are farmers who want this freedom, List 8 will have no objection. However, it is always important that it fits the scenic landscape of Winterswijk, and there must remain a good mix of functions in the countryside to keep it livable.

230872.explanation: List 8 believes sustainability should not be an end in itself. Imposing even more demands seems unnecessary. Common sense and a pragmatic view for and on companies seem more sensible to us. This applies to other business parks as well.

230873.explanation: Farmers have already been subjected to many rules. More rules are therefore completely unnecessary. More control from the farmer! Bottom-up is our way to guide the area process properly! For List 8, 'The farmer at the helm' is key.

230874.explanation: More overnight stays are not a goal in itself. However, List 8 believes the tourist season can be extended. A large-scale grab of the countryside is undesirable. Plans and changes must be well tested in the environment. Participation is key.

230875.explanation: List 8 believes we must work together with the chain to reduce waste volume. Moreover, you pay a disposal fee for many products, and other streams are raw materials again.

230876.explanation: When neighborhood matters are organized from citizen initiatives, List 8 believes something should be in return. If this saves on the municipal budget, we encourage it. If a contribution for, for example, the neighborhood barbecue is a solution, we are willing to provide it.

230877.explanation: List 8 feels that sustainability should not be an end in itself. Entrepreneurs often know best what is good for their businesses. Imposing more demands stalls development and does not contribute to a better business climate.

230878.explanation: Verge strips, paths, and roads must be well passable. Better maintenance and supervision are therefore necessary. List 8 believes safety comes first. A good and appropriate mowing policy will form the basis.

230879.explanation: List 8 wants to preserve agricultural land as much as possible for our food supply. There are many other solutions than solar fields and wind turbines to meet the energy demand.

230880.explanation: As far as List 8 is concerned, wind turbines should remain off the territory of Winterswijk at all times. Wind turbines do not fit into the rural area of Winterswijk. There are plenty of other solutions than solar fields and wind turbines to meet the energy demand.

230881.explanation: Closing the Market is not desirable in the current situation. First, good alternatives must be explored to keep the center easily accessible.

230882.explanation: List 8 has no fixed view on the exact form this should take. Multiple options will need to be considered. However, a day (bad weather) facility is a good addition. A contribution from the municipality may thus be necessary. It should not be a subsidized model by the municipality.

230883.explanation: The Whemerbeek already has a very nice open character, among other places in Scholtenbrugpark. List 8 finds this open structure more than sufficient. Otherwise, it should be left as it is.

230884.explanation: List 8 supports fewer regulations for farmers. A farmer often knows best how agriculture works. Bottom-up is our way to properly guide the area process! For List 8, the 'Farmer at the Helm' is key.

230885.explanation: Currently, waste separation can already be done much better by technology than by humans. List 8 therefore believes the current collection system should be overhauled. We do think less waste should be produced. This is a task for the entire chain. 'Don't bother the citizen.'

230886.explanation: Spending more money does not lead to better integration! Ultimately, it depends on the individual! Winterswijk has taken great responsibility in this area for decades. List 8 feels it is now the neighboring municipalities' turn! Apart from the fact that these issues need broader solutions.

230887.explanation: When companies turn to labor migration, the municipality must monitor this. Unfortunately, there is often no good housing arranged for this group. Also, companies may opt for robotization instead of cheap labor.

230888.explanation: Winterswijk far exceeds the national average; List 8 believes immigration should be better managed at Europe’s external borders. However, it can be considered whether the number Winterswijk hosts can be reduced.

230889.explanation: If it turns out the new national regulation is insufficient, the municipality may reconsider. List 8 believes RTV Slingeland must be well aligned with modern times. Our preference is for an East Achterhoek broadcaster!

230890.explanation: List 8 sees that Achterhoeks as a regional language is slowly disappearing. We will therefore encourage more attention for this. List 8 itself will submit a motion to hold council meetings at least once a year in the dialect. 'If you can speak the dialect, don’t let it go.'

230892.explanation: List 8 considers a swimming pool to be among the municipality’s basic facilities. Moreover, privatization has shown it is not always cheaper. The swimming pool belongs in the category of sport and culture.

230894.explanation: Good education is, for List 8, part of the municipality's basic care duty. If extra investment is needed here, we will contribute. We do believe education must suit the child and also allow free time.

230897.explanation: If establishing an additional coffeeshop counteracts illegal trade on the streets, List 8 is willing to adjust the existing policy accordingly.
