230898.explanation: Every road user showing antisocial behavior (including fatbikers) must be checked or fined. Additionally, more respect for the police in general is also a theme for List 8.

230899.explanation: Although public enforcement officers (boa’s) almost begin to resemble the municipal police of 'the past,' more respect is also a point of attention. They must be more visible on 'busy days' in the center. Maintaining good contact with shopkeepers and entrepreneurs as well as social contact in the neighborhood!

230900.explanation: A good facility for parking bicycles is obviously important. This could be a secured facility. Providing it with human surveillance seems a step too far to us.

230901.explanation: If shopkeepers want to choose to be open on Sundays, List 8 considers this part of entrepreneurial freedom. As long as it contributes to a lively and vibrant center, we support it. Let entrepreneurs with knowledge take the lead.

230902.explanation: Entrepreneurs may adapt their operations to customer wishes. If freedom of opening hours contributes to that, we will not oppose it. Of course, this must not cause nuisance to the surroundings. This is within the entrepreneur’s responsibility.

230903.explanation: We are against more camera surveillance. Of course, in exceptional cases it can provide a sense of security or prevent nuisance. However, we want to prevent false security in all cases.

230904.explanation: We want young people and seniors who want to stay or return to the municipality of Winterswijk to have priority for social rental housing. Of course, we are not against new Winterswijkers. They will eventually become one of us! 'But first our own.'

230905.explanation: List 8 believes we must adhere to our own agreements. 30% is the agreed norm. So no excuses or circumventing. Let’s do it!

230906.explanation: Investors must make their own considerations to convert certain buildings. List 8 believes the municipality can only facilitate changes. Additionally, there must be a clear and good assessment per area before any change of use takes place.

230907.explanation: Providing a temporary contribution to the housing shortage is negotiable. However, we want to retain the holiday home designation. When converting to residential use, the municipality is responsible for infrastructure and such.

230908.explanation: Infill always comes first. Only in exceptional cases and as a last resort will List 8 sacrifice agricultural land for housing and industry. Consultation with the surroundings must take place before this step. Participation!

230910.explanation: If the plot allows and certain conditions are met, List 8 supports this form of living. Whether it concerns 'pre' care, youth, or elderly housing, we will embrace these initiatives.

230911.explanation: The market must retain its open character. The market day, King's Day, Folk Festival, and other activities must keep their place on the Market. More trees and shrubs could hinder these activities.

230912.explanation: Ultimately, Winterswijk will have to look for a new location with larger expansion. This will need further investigation. The crossing at Rondweg-West will also need extra attention! List 8 believes this location is better suited for business activity.

230913.explanation: Attention to this target group is essential. Unfortunately, poverty is becoming increasingly common. Life has become more expensive. Raising the limit can be among the options. People in poverty must be supported.

230914.explanation: If a meaningful reciprocal contribution can be provided in an appropriate manner, List 8 sees this as a good option. 'Socially meaningful' work connects and fosters social participation.

230915.explanation: Ensure people can go to work. More attention must be given here. People need better support to find 'real' work. List 8 believes the municipality should not try to organize a second Hameland.

230916.explanation: If people can no longer afford these things, we will have to provide care here. List 8 also believes there must be good support. Waiving debts should not be an aim in itself.

230917.explanation: If this retraining leads to work, it could offer a temporary solution. List 8 believes that if applying for jobs is application for its own sake, it has little added value in our view.

230920.explanation: Winterswijk only facilitates the possibility of placements. For List 8, we do not need to lead in this regard.

230922.explanation: 30 km/h is not an end in itself for List 8. We believe roads should be designed to suit the traffic that uses them. Emergency services' response times must also be taken into account.

230923.explanation: A good connection between both sides of the railway is an important point of attention for List 8. The connection on both sides of the track needs a thorough makeover!

230924.explanation: A tunnel is not the cheapest solution. However, this will solve a big problem for List 8, and a broader consideration will be made to choose this option. The connection on both sides of the railway needs a thorough makeover!

230925.explanation: There must be a look at a better accessible center. List 8 believes the center is currently not easily reachable. This might mean that Vrijheidspark could offer a solution.

230926.explanation: 30 km/h is not an end in itself for List 8. We believe roads should be designed to suit the traffic that uses them. Emergency services' response times must also be taken into account.

230928.explanation: Winterswijk must primarily obtain better access on the short term via the west (SKB) side. When new neighborhoods are developed, better attention must be paid to good accessibility. List 8 believes that others should not have to pay the price or become the victims.

230929.explanation: We prefer to invest in nightlife for young people in Winterswijk. List 8 would like to work together with young people to find a solid foundation for various recreational activities. We want to connect young people to Winterswijk.

230930.explanation: Ultimately, we are against drug use, but to better understand which substances are circulating, List 8 supports a drug testing location. This way we can monitor and provide better education.

230931.explanation: As the municipality of Winterswijk, we do not need to implement policy on this. Hospitality businesses may decide for themselves whether to adopt this in their operations. List 8 will not impose additional rules here.

230932.explanation: Awareness is a theme that belongs to the core values of List 8. Ultimately, a healthy offering in stores should be the norm for us! Shopping locally at the farmer's, knowing where your food comes from, and cooking lessons at school will be encouraged.

230933.explanation: Caring for others can sometimes be a heavy task. List 8 wants to relieve caregivers at certain moments. Replacement care can contribute to the caregiver’s well-being. All this to reduce the overall burden on healthcare.

parties.name: ORDA / ORP
parties.fullName: Oranje Republikeinse Partij Democratische Alliantie / Oranje Republikeinse Piraten

230864.explanation: Absolutely agree. The main points from this questionnaire and issues in the municipality should be presented at least once a year, for example during elections, with periodic binding referenda. That is one of the key pillars of the principles of ORDA / ORP in politics.

230865.explanation: At present, there seem to be too many civil servants in the whole of the Netherlands, not too few. Maybe more volunteers working at food banks should receive compensation or other organizations be established for a fairer and more just society, also in the neighborhoods of the municipality.

230866.explanation: This needs further examination. There must be an elected mayor, and perhaps, like in Antwerp, even two mayors at the current salary. Also, the on-call service and clerk's office must be better accessible during all office hours, including Friday afternoons, which is currently not the case.

230867.explanation: There should be no youth council at this time, as it will likely be corrupted and used. Perhaps youth centers like Elbekurkie in Meddo and the old Eucalypta with stages and activities should return with subsidies and certainly reduce regulatory burden on events.

230868.explanation: First, the number of civil servants must be investigated. Dysfunctioning civil servants should be sanctioned earlier, especially if they unlawfully violate the rights of residents of the municipality or Dutch citizens with self-invented rules, as often happens now. Overall, fewer civil servants in the Netherlands.

230869.explanation: Spending more money on campaigns is wasted money. Keeping taxes stable or lowering them is better for all Dutch people, who are already taxed too much compared to other European countries.

230870.explanation: One shopping Sunday is sufficient; Sunday rest and other recreational matters are also important.

230871.explanation: Almost always agree. A small campsite should be possible, for example with a maximum of 5 tents or 2 caravans and no nuisance caused. Preserving tourism, rural economy, and farmers is important for ORDA / ORP. Without farmers, no food.

230872.explanation: The question is what is sustainable and whether the Arrisveld industrial site is necessary or not. That should be investigated first and perhaps decided by a referendum among the residents of Winterswijk, according to ORDA / Orange Republican Pirates.

230873.explanation: NGOs and other political stakeholders are trying too much to gain power over the countryside and farmers, so farmers must get more freedom and be freed from nitrogen issues, a problem invented by the elite.

230874.explanation: The number of tourists may be increased for Winterswijk. That is better than bringing immigrants from outside Europe in asylum centers who only come to collect money. Tourists bring sociability and income for the economy, shopping center, as well as businesses.

230875.explanation: Some items should be able to be disposed of for free, and for some things payment is necessary. Otherwise, we will get waste tourism from abroad to here, as already happens from Naples or Italy to the Amsterdam area for financial gain, leaving us with mountains of waste from, for example, Italy.

230876.explanation: The municipality and government may reward residents with small subsidies for volunteer work and tasks like maintaining greenery in their neighborhood. This also happens in Utrecht and should definitely occur in rural areas with much more nature.

230877.explanation: The question that must be asked is what is meant nowadays by sustainable? Companies must not all cause waste, toxins, asbestos, or nuisance as Chemours in Dordrecht or the Hoogovens in IJmuiden do. However, money must be earned and jobs are needed for people.

230878.explanation: Research must come first; therefore, ORDA / ORP has no exact position on this yet.

230879.explanation: Agricultural land is more important than solar panels of the elite, which currently also prove to be a disaster for the environment along with wind turbines, especially at sea and for whales and other nature and animal species.

230880.explanation: Winterswijk, the Achterhoek, and Gelderland must also look at what they can do against wind turbines just across the border in Germany and other such unwanted facilities causing nuisance to neighborhoods and support the action and neighborhood groups established for this purpose.

230881.explanation: Closing the Vrijheidspark was already a major disaster and it must be reopened for traffic, delivery trucks for the shopping center, and also for fire brigade, ambulance, and police, such as in cases of heart attacks or other incidents. The recent fire on the Markt was noticed in the center.

230882.explanation: The costs and benefits must be studied first. In principle, ORDA / ORP supports more tourist recreational activities in Winterswijk, as well as more events, and therefore less regulatory burden for events such as autocross, Easter fires, fairs and folk festivals, or tent parties and youth chains.

230883.explanation: Nature and streams are beautiful, but this is nonsense and wasted money. Winterswijk has one of the best shopping centers in Gelderland and it should remain so. It is good as it is now. Only the density of shops and hospitality must remain optimal and be monitored with current internet sales.

230884.explanation: We are not opposed to organic or other farmers and also support preserving small farms. So the latter should certainly be supported, but this position must first be further investigated by ORDA / ORP.

230885.explanation: There are already too many fines. Let police, judiciary, CJIB, and judges first tackle corruption, blackmail, and other more important issues in the Netherlands, The Hague, Amsterdam, Rotterdam, Utrecht and especially in Brussels, system banks, the European Commission, and the elite, not the population.

230886.explanation: No more asylum centers or immigrants from outside Europe should come to the Achterhoek. Immigrants are also responsible for immigration themselves and should possibly contribute or work to be part of Dutch society. Furthermore, we support regional shelter, like Pim Fortuyn.

230887.explanation: Some Polish or Eastern European labor immigrants are important, but they must have rights and good incomes or shelter and should not be given priority over Dutch people who do want to work and cannot get jobs due to cheap Poles or other labor immigrants.

230888.explanation: This must be further investigated. The Winterswijk asylum center has existed for ages or at least a very long time. Capacity should in any case not increase and the asylum center must be well-organized and controlled. Furthermore, ORDA is against more asylum centers in the Netherlands or the Achterhoek.

230889.explanation: Winterswijk supports the preservation of regional media, but the reporting must be honest, and freedom of expression as well as freedom of journalism must exist. That also means no cover-ups, slanderous defamatory publications, such as currently happens in mainstream media.

230890.explanation: The Achterhoek dialect and the Low Saxon traditions, history, and customs should receive some attention in primary schools and sometimes also in secondary schools for awareness among children. The dialect does not need to return. Currently, standard Dutch (ABN) is more practical across the country.

230892.explanation: Swimming pool Jaspers is a nice pool. It must be run well and as optimally as possible and seems dependent on subsidies, so it should remain under municipal control at this time.

230894.explanation: It seems to us that children already go to school enough and the workload for teachers is already very high, as are today’s demands. Smaller classes, more teachers, and no propaganda, as well as renewed attention to math, language, and other basics are more important, and smartphones and computers should be limited.

230897.explanation: ORDA / ORP strives for a certain legalization of certain substances, like for hash/weed and ecstasy. Also better education and control of ecstasy and MDMA pills beforehand. ORDA / ORP sees some room for a coffee shop, but not in the village center.

230898.explanation: Especially in cities, things get out of hand with fatbikes and antisocial riding behavior, where you are simply run over at high speeds. The question is how these people — sometimes very young people or even asylum seekers — can afford such fatbikes? Research and control are needed.

230899.explanation: Community enforcement officers (BOAs) mainly cause nuisance, perhaps only useful in places like Utrecht's station. BOAs are only necessary in big cities; in rural areas, perhaps a forest ranger or community monitoring with good neighborliness is better.

230900.explanation: Too many bicycles are often stolen at the station or elsewhere, and a bicycle parking facility with perhaps a public toilet may — after cost-benefit analysis — be good for the shopping center and reduce car and traffic pressure while providing safe bicycle parking.

230901.explanation: Not at this moment. Maybe some shopping Sundays after consultation and special request if there is demand. Therefore, currently neither for nor against this position.

230902.explanation: In the past, Winterswijk had a weekend closing time of 2:00 AM for pubs and nightclubs. That was somewhat ridiculous and detrimental to events and the youth demographic, who wanted to experience something on weekends and therefore went elsewhere, like Groenlo or German clubs.

230903.explanation: ORDA / ORP opposes increased camera surveillance and fights against a Global Prison Planet, including in Winterswijk where, in our view, such surveillance is currently unnecessary. Such monitoring should only be applied, possibly temporarily, in problematic areas using cameras.

230904.explanation: Agreed. Local residents should be given priority, especially over people from outside the EU or even Europe, rather than the other way around — where Achterhoek residents and Dutch people pay everything, while safe-country migrants benefit and simply choose the cheapest countries, meanwhile whistleblowers have no rights left.

230905.explanation: Social housing is certainly important in large towns and cities, but also in Winterswijk due to excessively high mortgages. Although in Achterhoek, owner-occupied homes are more important, and therefore thirty percent social rental housing is a reasonable percentage.

230906.explanation: Vacant offices should be repurposed, while shops might need subsidies to maintain a lively and attractive center if too much vacancy threatens. Shops don't necessarily have to be converted, but it is permitted if owners want to.

230907.explanation: For a certain percentage, due to high mortgage costs, housing shortage, and other issues like homelessness, this should be allowed, including for young people. However, vacation homes for tourists must remain, and possibly more should be built in areas like Hilgelo or the Rommel Mountains or elsewhere.

230908.explanation: Construction should also be possible in hamlets like Kotten, Meddo, Corle or outside the built-up area of Winterswijk, and even proceed if ORDA / ORP supports it and locals in those hamlets or the village want it.

230910.explanation: The municipality can allow this, but it depends on the kind of garden. A garden shed should be allowed if it looks good and is quality-made, but a home might only be allowed in the countryside and on farms.

230911.explanation: Trees and bushes currently only get in the way of market vendors and the weekly market and events. They also only cost money and maintenance, yet the market has looked good in Winterswijk in recent decades.

230912.explanation: Temporarily agreed. More and relatively affordable homes should be built for young people and the native Dutch population if needed. The housing shortage in the Netherlands, with many native homeless people in recent years, is immense and must be addressed by building social rental housing, among other measures.

230913.explanation: Poverty must be more actively combated, as well as the banking elite. This can be done by introducing the Zucman tax for individuals with over 100 million euros in assets and other measures such as a form of nationalization of system banks into cooperative system banks with ownership shares for every Dutch citizen.

230914.explanation: Research must be done on basic income in Achterhoek, possibly partly conditional and partly with a work obligation, for example working in the countryside or with farmers as assistance for better mutual understanding if farmers agree. Also, a small amount of additional income should be allowed with social assistance, as is the case in Utrecht.

230915.explanation: This requires investigation first, so ORDA / ORP has no position yet. Unnecessary menial jobs or unnecessary enforcement officers or civil servants should not be created, but in certain cases, work and jobs can be subsidized and stimulated.

230916.explanation: People with low incomes must also have rights. There must be a fairer distribution and less bureaucracy, and taxes should be reduced or at least not increased due to all sorts of expenses like a freedom tax, purchasing farms for nitrogen reduction, etc.

230917.explanation: Studying to apply for a new job or to acquire new talents should be rewarded or at least not penalized.

230920.explanation: The EU's regulatory zeal and pressure from other organizations with the fiasco of electric cars is excessive. First, research should establish how many charging points will actually be needed in the future and whether fuel or hybrid cars might be much better for freedom, nature, environment, and other matters.

230922.explanation: 50 kilometer per hour roads are fine.

230923.explanation: It's better to reduce taxes than to have a project that isn't necessarily needed. Only if funds are available and a good plan exists, and the project is not more important than other matters, should it possibly be considered here. Perhaps the accessibility via the railway station should be improved elsewhere.

230924.explanation: It's better to reduce taxes than to have a project that isn't necessarily needed. Only if funds are available and a good plan exists, and the project is not more important than other matters, should it possibly be considered here. Perhaps the accessibility via the railway station should be improved elsewhere.

230925.explanation: If necessary, with a 30-kilometer road and one-way traffic. The problem must be solved, improved, and restored as soon as possible for center access and traffic, as well as for delivery of goods to shops and the market, and for ambulances, fire brigade, and police.

230926.explanation: A 50 km/h speed limit for Vredenseweg is fine as a maximum. Other roads in the outskirts have proven much more dangerous over the past decades.

230928.explanation: Currently, there appears to be no need for this.

230929.explanation: This needs first to be researched. Perhaps a single night train is needed on Fridays or Saturdays for youths who want to go out, but probably with proper supervision and control.

230930.explanation: Test locations must be established in Achterhoek, as well as better education about dangerous substances like heroin, crystal meth, and especially cocaine, XTC, and MDMA use, although we think this is more relevant in larger cities like Amsterdam with its old good house parties.

230931.explanation: Smoking outdoors should simply be allowed, provided cigarette butts are cleaned up and disposed of in trash bins.

230932.explanation: This should simply be done by dietitians, general practitioners, hospitals, and in health care education at secondary schools.

230933.explanation: Informal caregivers are often overburdened, and for family and elderly, washing each other is not pleasant. This leads to shame and other (work) pressure. Therefore, replacement care must always be available if possible and should be financed.

230864.explanation: Citizen participation is important. However, a referendum can cost a lot of money and time and delay decisions. We see municipal elections as the most important moment for residents to provide direction. Therefore, we especially focus on good information and direct contact with citizens.

230865.explanation: Make more proactive use of an 'energy coach' who relieves and advises. To minimize the hassle of moving so that everyone is happy to move, we also believe a municipal 'moving coach' should be used more proactively to relieve and advise.

230866.explanation: Building bridges. This starts by always first listening to what the people of Winterswijk have to say about certain matters. You can only achieve this with as much personal contact as possible. Civil servants and aldermen must therefore get out more. Four aldermen are necessary for this.

230867.explanation: Daadkracht.nu is the youth party! Led by Jordi Gebbink (29 years) and Oliver Heming (20 years). Daadkracht.nu's ideal is to breathe new life into youth events in Winterswijk. The municipality should dare to invest or provide financial support without too many restrictions.

230868.explanation: The municipality spends too much time and money on plans, layers of policies, and external advisors. This comes at the expense of implementation. We focus explicitly not on people but on minimizing systems. By organizing smarter and better utilizing knowledge, it can be more efficient and humane.

230869.explanation: Preferably invest a lot of energy in increasing the attractiveness of existing business parks. Only a few companies wish to expand or relocate in the future. Most Winterswijk companies want to stay put. The focus should be there.

230870.explanation: Allowing more Sunday shopping days per year leads to a shift of shopping time from Saturday to Sunday, which is undesirable considering the pull of the Saturday market (especially for Germans). Without that market, Winterswijk is only moderately attractive on Sundays.

230871.explanation: "Always" is too easy. Of course, we support local initiatives and entrepreneurship to generate extra income and extend the season. But always fitting the Winterswijk scale: both in size (infill rather than expansion) and financially.

230872.explanation: No new industrial area! Focus energies on making existing business parks more attractive. Few companies want to expand or relocate. Most Winterswijk companies want to remain where they are.

230873.explanation: We commit to strengthening nature together with farms. We seek new sustainable and lasting income models for farmers and encourage new entrepreneurship in the countryside. Removing barriers, extra rules, or paper tigers is a priority.

230874.explanation: Give entrepreneurs room to create quality (bad weather) facilities to be a first-class tourist attraction throughout the season. Always fitting within the 'Winterswijk scale'. Extending the season is a better option than increasing visitor numbers currently.

230875.explanation: Stimulating waste separation is done by making it easier and removing barriers. Free disposal of consumer waste certainly motivates.

230876.explanation: This increases neighborhood involvement but in practice means a limited number of volunteers do all the work. By keeping management in-house, the municipality can better guarantee quality, safety, and liability.

230877.explanation: We primarily focus on existing local businesses that fit Winterswijk in nature and scale: many people on few square meters. So, manufacturing is welcome, but not more transport, logistics, or trade.

230878.explanation: By mowing carefully, you create space for plants and animals (biodiversity). For traffic safety, the sightlines at intersections, bends, and the first 60 cm along the road are now mowed twice a year—once in spring and once in summer. That is sufficient.

230879.explanation: Not into the meadows, but up onto the roofs with solar panels. The solar ladder is meant to prevent solar panels being placed on agricultural and natural land. Plenty of solar panels on farm roofs and commercial buildings. Generate 75% sustainable energy by installing another 400,000 solar panels on roofs.

230880.explanation: No! Onshore wind energy is definitely not preferred. "It doesn’t blow enough here." This obviously also applies to wind turbines (249 meters high!) at the border with the Netherlands. Generating sustainable energy through indirect participation in offshore wind farms in the North Sea is a much better idea. At least there it’s windy.

230881.explanation: Entrepreneurs in Winterswijk are overwhelmingly against the plan to close the Winterswijk Market to traffic and parking for good reasons. The loss of 66 parking spaces at the Market and Torenstraat will be disastrous for customer visits on all days except Wednesday morning and Saturday.

230882.explanation: Daadkracht.nu sees little future for museums with a high knit-cap factor, but definitely for modern and attractively designed experience centers. At locations that have a direct connection with those topics. So let’s speed up the Steengroeve Museum, right at the quarry itself. Naturally.

230883.explanation: We cannot afford to lose any parking spots in the center. That is what makes Winterswijk so appealing: free and nearby parking. Daadkracht therefore does not want to bring the Whemerbeek above ground at the site of the Wheme and Blikbak (that is, on the north side of Zonnebrink/Dingstraat).

230884.explanation: The establishment of a landscape fund containing an easy-to-apply reward system—such as the Marke model—so that farmers can develop activities related to landscape, culture, and cultural history. We commit ourselves to strengthening nature together with the farming enterprises.

230885.explanation: In the Netherlands, we are obliged to separate waste. This way, we contribute together to a circular economy in which as many products and materials as possible are reused. Make sure waste goes into the correct container. That way, you won’t get fined.

230886.explanation: Current investments should shift much more toward activating newcomers in language, social participation, but especially labor participation. Because this yields long-term benefits such as less poverty and more well-being.

230887.explanation: The Netherlands has approximately 1.7 million labor migrants, more than twice as many as previously thought. Too many still live and work in poor conditions. Some employers and agencies exploit their vulnerable position. Control and enforcement are absolutely necessary.

230888.explanation: Winterswijk has an indefinite contract with the COA for refugee accommodation. COA rents the buildings from owner De Woonplaats. Daadkracht.nu wants to agree on an end date for the asylum seekers’ center. So we have control over what we do in the future with these homes intended for social rental housing!

230889.explanation: RTV Slingeland is not just any broadcaster but the voice and face of Winterswijk. The volunteers capture our community: the corso, the associations, the entrepreneurs, and the stories that connect us. Without local support, that recognition disappears. So support it.

230890.explanation: Good education starts with a solid foundation in Dutch language, math, and history. The weekly hours primary schools spend on reading will increase from one to five hours with us. Dialect is valuable and belongs in culture and identity but must not add extra pressure to the curriculum.

230892.explanation: We have proven for years that we cannot get the budget balanced. Millions more will be needed for Jaspers, structurally. Then selling to a private party seems like the best option.

230894.explanation: Back to language, math, history. The number of weekly hours that primary schools must spend on reading increases from 1 to 5 hours. We believe that will make a big difference, even in Winterswijk’s weakest schools.

230897.explanation: Coffeeshop Sensi is still the only coffeeshop in Winterswijk and causes no nuisance. The current situation therefore gives no reason to change the policy substantially.

230898.explanation: Absolutely necessary! Daadkracht.nu supports a total ban on fatbikes.

230899.explanation: Municipalities like Winterswijk (about 29,000 inhabitants) often use an average norm of about one enforcement officer per 3,500 residents. That seems sufficient to us.

230900.explanation: Necessary due to the increase in the number of e-bikes.

230901.explanation: Allowing more Sunday shopping days per year leads to a shift of shopping time from Saturday to Sunday, which is undesirable considering the pull of the Saturday market (especially for Germans). Without that market, Winterswijk is only moderately attractive on Sundays.

230902.explanation: Hospitality entrepreneurs cannot fully determine their opening hours freely. They are bound by local regulations (General Local Regulation) and policies that set fixed windows (minimum) and closing times (maximum), aiming to safeguard public order, safety, and the residential living environment.

230903.explanation: Municipalities use camera surveillance to reduce crime in the center, such as persistent nuisance by loitering youth, drug dealers, street robbers, and pickpockets. We believe this is currently not necessary.

230904.explanation: Winterswijk must specify in the housing regulations that housing seekers with local ties (living, working, care) get priority for up to 50% of social rental and owner-occupied homes. Because there is scarcity, this is a necessity. We must seize every opportunity to help Winterswijk residents find housing.

230905.explanation: To improve the financial feasibility of new construction projects for contractors/builders, the requirement should be lowered to a maximum of 23%. Currently, only 17% of homes here are rental via the housing corporation, and 70% are owner-occupied. Apparently, we prefer buying to renting in Winterswijk.

230906.explanation: Fast permit granting for all initiatives to split vacant offices, shops, larger buildings, and/or homes into smaller residential units (also naturally in rural areas). Outbuildings on private land may also more easily obtain a residential function.

230907.explanation: We must seize every opportunity to help Winterswijk residents find housing.

230908.explanation: Fast permit granting for all initiatives to split larger buildings and/or homes into smaller living units. Of course also in rural areas, on private property. Only 16% of all municipalities encourage splitting of homes; 84% do not.

230910.explanation: Outbuildings on private land may more easily obtain a residential function. Also, guidelines and indications for yet-to-be-built pre-caregiver homes on private land should be much more flexible. Fast permit granting for all initiatives to split larger buildings and/or homes.

230911.explanation: Winterswijk’s shopping center is becoming increasingly green. The greening of Wooldstraat, Willinkstraat, and Satinksplas has received positive feedback. Following multiple streets, we also have the Market on the list to be addressed. It will create a more attractive shopping area.

230912.explanation: By creating 375 ‘Happy Senior Apartments,’ we hope to motivate 550 currently still vital Winterswijk residents to exchange their current family homes. Those 550 fit seniors represent only 7.5% of the current 7,356 Winterswijk residents aged over 65. That means 375 extra (owner-occupied) homes available.

230913.explanation: Don’t wait. Proactively address the cause in time. So better to look at the cause (why poverty?) than just solve the effects (aid). Raising the income threshold for aid is too easy.

230914.explanation: Everyone feels best when they contribute. It is therefore not just an exchange, but rather an attractive way to give meaning to your life.

230915.explanation: Don’t wait. Proactively address the cause in time. So better to look at the cause (why long-term unemployment?) than just solve the effects (subsidies for jobs).

230916.explanation: Don’t wait. Proactively address the cause in time. So better to look at the cause (why a low income?) than just solve the effects (municipal tax debt). Forgiving debts is too easy.

230917.explanation: Applying for jobs takes relatively little time. People on welfare should be able to spend that time themselves without too much delay. So do it! Get to work, look for employment.

230920.explanation: Winterswijk has 128 charging points. According to Daadkracht.nu, that number should be doubled.

230922.explanation: The policy is: 30 km/h 'unless'... Groenloseweg in Winterswijk was narrowed by about one meter and equipped with speed bumps so road users will comply with the 30 km/h limit. We will first wait for the evaluation before implementing 30 km/h on other roads.

230923.explanation: There will be no tunnel or crossing in Winterswijk to connect Dingstraat with Parallelweg. That would cost at least 25 million euros. Research also shows that traffic flow would not improve. “So don’t do it,” says Daadkracht.nu!

230924.explanation: There will be no tunnel or crossing in Winterswijk to connect Dingstraat with Parallelweg. That would cost at least 25 million euros. Research also shows that traffic flow would not improve. “So don’t do it,” says Daadkracht.nu!

230925.explanation: Daadkracht.nu wants to include Vrijheidspark when drafting scenarios/design sketches for the Market and Torenstraat to improve traffic flow there. Or to consider opening a road through Vrijheidspark with better traffic flow throughout the entire center as a result.

230926.explanation: The policy is: 30 km/h 'unless'... Groenloseweg in Winterswijk was narrowed by about one meter and equipped with speed bumps so road users will comply with the 30 km/h limit. We will first wait for the evaluation before implementing 30 km/h on other roads.

230928.explanation: There is no necessity for that. What problem would this solve? Most traffic comes and goes from Winterswijk to Aalten, Lichtenvoorde, and Groenlo. There is already the Rondweg-West there. Moreover, this would result in dozens of procedures involving expropriation and such. We shouldn’t want that.

230929.explanation: Who needs this? If it were interesting for travelers or profitable for the carrier, NS or Arriva would have come up with this idea already.

230930.explanation: Obviously not. So that we definitely facilitate people to use drugs? Not doing that.

230931.explanation: Smoking is permitted on the outdoor terrace unless you, as the owner of the hospitality location, decide to prohibit it. You have the option to decide that smoking on the premises of your hospitality location is no longer desirable. This can be indicated by signs that designate the location as a smoke-free area.

230932.explanation: Nutrition education: this concerns educating students and promoting a sustainable food culture that delivers long-term benefits for the entire country. One nutritionist per 550 students, or one per 1500 students at schools with a central kitchen. That is the wish of Daadkracht.nu.

230933.explanation: We will definitely not put even more pressure on the self-reliance of people who can manage on their own. On the contrary, we have confidence in the own strength of residents and in the Winterswijk community and its organizational capacity. But we will not further overburden informal caregivers.

statements.title: Vrijheidspark should be reopened to cars.
statements.moreInfo: The Vrijheidspark is the area in front of the Town Hall. This area is currently not accessible to cars because of two large blocks.

People who sort their waste poorly should be fined.

There should be more overnight accommodations for tourists in Winterswijk.
Winterswijk should support the local broadcaster RTV Slingeland with additional funding.

titleParts.text: RTV Slingeland ondersteunen met extra geld.

statements.title: Housing may be built on the Tuunterveld.
statements.moreInfo: The Tuunterveld is an area on the edge of Winterswijk at the roundabout of Rondweg-West and Europalaan.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
At least 30% of the newly built homes in Winterswijk should be social rental housing.

statements.moreInfo: At the moment only the west and south sides of Winterswijk are served by a ring road.

titleParts.glossary: Unpaid and often long-term care for sick family members or friends.

statements.title: People on social assistance who are taking additional training should temporarily not have to apply for jobs.

statements.title: The residents of Winterswijk should be able to express their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Winterswijk moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.moreInfo: In a referendum a government puts a single-issue question to voters, who can vote for or against. In a municipal referendum all residents of that municipality who are 18 or older may vote. Both members of the municipal council and residents can request a referendum. If residents want a referendum, they must collect signatures. The municipal rules state how many signatures are required. In the Netherlands the result of a referendum is not binding. The municipality does not have to follow the result and can make a different decision.

statements.title: The speed limit in built-up areas should be lowered everywhere to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
